# context-radar

> A comparison catalogue of tools that reduce context-window token
> consumption in Claude Code and comparable coding agents (Codex, OpenCode,
> Cursor, Gemini CLI). Each entry answers: does this tool reduce what gets
> loaded into or generated within an agent's context window, and how does it
> interact with the rest of the stack? 79 tools, last updated 2026-07-15.

## Data

- Source of truth: data/context-reduction-tools.csv (14 columns, one row per tool)
- JSON mirror: data/context-reduction-tools.json ({meta, tools:[...]})
- Star history (append-only): data/star-history.csv
- Methodology: skills/project-comparison-fetch/SKILL.md

## Conflict markers

- HARD conflict: two MCP servers expose the same or near-identical tool name; an agent cannot route between them. Pick one.
- SOFT conflict: redundant role or overlap; running both wastes resources but does not break routing.

## Verdict vocabulary

Best in class, Add, Add if you use [X], Either/or pick one, Watch (too early / unique but early), Reference only (not a tool), Drop.

## Catalogue by layer (19 layers)

Layers and their order match the display layers in the comparison table (docs/index.html).

### Shell & tool output compression — pick exactly one shell tool

- [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) (Best in class; 71,000 stars): Rust hook rewrites Bash cmds; compresses git/test/lint output. 90+ patterns. No Bun yet.
- [sqz](https://github.com/ojuschugh1/sqz) (Either/or; 529 stars): RTK superset: SHA-256 dedup cache (repeat reads = 13 tokens), TOON JSON, null stripping, MCP server (sqz-mcp), predictive pre-cache. v1.0.9. npm install or brew install or curl installer.
- [lean-ctx](https://github.com/yvgude/lean-ctx) (Best in class features; 3,200 stars): Shell hook + 76 MCP tools (Property Graph, CCP cross-session memory, filesystem guard, provenance store). Bun-native, local-first. 60-90% fewer tokens. 30+ agents. v3.x. brew/npm/cargo/curl install.
- [omni](https://github.com/fajarhide/omni) (Add; 300 stars): Rust "Context Operating System". Intercepts terminal commands, streams output through declarative TOML rule-based distillers (no LLM calls). Adaptive compression self-tunes based on omni_retrieve frequency. Smart bypass skips distillation under 2000-token outputs. RewindStore: zero info loss, raw output retrievable via omni_retrieve. Native MCP server (omni mcp, stdio, no subprocess latency). Engrams: automatic rule-based subtask digests (no LLM cost) preventing context amnesia. Context pressure traffic light (omni_insight, warns at 65%/82% thresholds). Goal-driven constraint scaling (debug retains errors, refactor compresses code). Multi-agent collaboration via OMNI_AGENT_ID — Cursor/Claude/Hermes share filtered memory streams. Session handoffs (omni_handoff). Lightweight dependency graph at hook time. v0.6.0, 42 releases, migrated from Node/Zig to Rust.
- [agent-context-economy](https://github.com/grafikerdem/agent-context-economy) (Watch; 15 stars): Six PowerShell scripts the agent calls instead of raw commands to reduce context bloat. run-compact.ps1: runs noisy builds/tests, feeds only error summary to agent (-93% terminal lines). read-symbol.ps1: grabs only a named function/class/component by name instead of reading whole file. read-window.ps1: reads a precise line range. investigate.ps1: batches multiple search patterns into one structured report (-71% shell commands). find-in-file.ps1: fast literal search in known file. diff-summary.ps1: ultra-compact git status. Agent rules in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md instruct agent to use these scripts automatically. PowerShell 7.0+. v0.1.1, 14 commits.
- [ctxlite](https://github.com/ctxlite/ctxlite) (Watch; 5 stars): Token optimizer spanning OpenCode, Cursor, Claude Code, and Claude Desktop in one project. Techniques: quieter Bash flags + blocked low-signal reads before a tool call, output compression + symbol-only file reads after, duplicate/stale context pruning before each request, BM25-based relevant-file selection (trim_context/smart_read MCP tools), conciseness instructions for model output. Per-platform automatic-vs-on-demand disclosed explicitly: OpenCode fully automatic (plugin + sidebar TUI widget); Claude Code automatic via PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks + on-demand MCP; Cursor pre-call rewrite automatic only (platform hook API cannot rewrite output for built-in tools, so output compression has no automatic path); Claude Desktop MCP-only (no hook/plugin API). .ctxliteignore (gitignore-style) blocks project-specific paths from reads/trim_context. 4 packages: @ctxlite/opencode, @ctxlite/mcp, @ctxlite/core (shared BM25 + tree-sitter symbol extraction), @ctxlite/cli. Unified stats at ~/.ctxlite/stats.db across all tools/platforms — npx @ctxlite/cli stats --last 7d --export json. Legacy Go HTTP proxy (L1/L2 caching) explicitly superseded by current plugin/MCP/hooks architecture. 74 commits, 7 tags.
- [context-mode](https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode) (Add; 19,000 stars): Sandboxes all tool output + session continuity across compaction
- [token-optimizer-mcp](https://github.com/ooples/token-optimizer-mcp) (Watch; 437 stars): MCP server replacing standard Claude Code file tools with cached/compressed equivalents. 61 tools: smart_read (serves diffs after first read, ~80% reduction on repeated reads), smart_grep, smart_glob, optimize_text (cache large content externally to remove from context), cache_warmup (SQLite pre-warm on session start), get_session_stats, monitoring dashboard. SQLite-based persistent cross-session cache. Brotli compression (2-4x typical, up to 82x repetitive content). Claims: 60-90% token reduction across 38,000+ operations. v5.0.1, 31 releases.

### Static context injection

- [sigmap](https://github.com/manojmallick/sigmap) (Add; 596 stars): Extracts fn/class signatures → CLAUDE.md passively. Zero deps. 31 languages. Graph-boosted retrieval (80% hit@5). 97.9% token reduction across 21 repos. 9 MCP tools. v6.10.
- [codesight](https://github.com/Houseofmvps/codesight) (Watch; 1,200 stars): Zero-dep npx CLI. Writes routes/schema/components/env/dep-graph as structured markdown in one command. 8 MCP tools with session caching. Blast radius via BFS. 5 AI tool profiles (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, codex.md). Watch mode, git hook, GitHub Action. 25+ framework detectors, 8 ORM parsers. v1.3.1.
- [codemap](https://github.com/JordanCoin/codemap) (Add; 642 stars): Go binary daemon. Budget-aware context at session start/edit/stop via Claude Code hooks. Cross-agent handoff artifacts (layered prefix/delta JSON with cache reuse metrics). Diff mode with hub impact warnings. Dependency flow via ast-grep. Remote repo analysis. Routing/subsystem config. Drift detection. Skyline visualisation. v4.0.7. 43 releases.
- [openwolf](https://github.com/cytostack/openwolf) (Add; 2,100 stars): 6 Claude Code lifecycle hooks creating .wolf/: anatomy.md (file map + token estimates), cerebrum.md (Do-Not-Repeat list + preferences), buglog.json (searchable fix memory), memory.md (action log), token-ledger.json (lifetime tracking). Blocks repeated file reads mid-session. designqc command captures screenshots for design review. reframe command for UI framework migrations. v1.0.4.
- [lat.md](https://github.com/1st1/lat.md) (Add; 1,800 stars): Markdown knowledge graph for codebases stored in a lat.md/ directory. Interconnected .md files document architecture, business logic, design decisions, and test specs — linked via [[wiki links]] to each other and to source code symbols (// @lat: [[section-id]] in code). Agents navigate via lat search (semantic), lat section, lat locate, lat refs, lat expand (expands [[refs]] inline without loading full graph). lat check enforces referential integrity between docs and code. Test spec enforcement: require-code-mention: true flags specs without code backlinks. lat mcp starts MCP server. lat init scaffolds directory and installs hooks in Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode. Agents maintain the graph; humans review semantic diffs in lat.md/ before code diffs. 21 releases, v0.11.0 Mar 2026. Author: Yury Selivanov (Python asyncio, uvloop, EdgeDB).

### Conversation history management

- [virtual-context](https://github.com/virtual-context/virtual-context) (Watch; 58 stars): OS virtual-memory model for message history: tag-based paging, IDF retrieval
- [magic-context](https://github.com/cortexkit/magic-context) (Add; 1,300 stars): OpenCode plugin. Background historian compresses older conversation into compartments + facts while main agent keeps working. Cache-aware queued reductions (never busts prompt cache). Cross-session project memory (architecture decisions, preferences). Dreamer: overnight agent consolidates/verifies memories, maintains ARCHITECTURE.md. User memory: developer behavioural observations promoted across sessions. Key file pinning. 5 agent tools (ctx_reduce, ctx_expand, ctx_note, ctx_memory, ctx_search). TUI sidebar. Desktop app. v0.15.7. 72 releases. ⚠ OpenCode-only — does not work with Claude Code.

### Personal knowledge retrieval

- [qmd](https://github.com/tobi/qmd) (Add; 28,000 stars): Local hybrid search: BM25 + vector + LLM reranking over your own notes/docs. Bun-native.
- [iwe](https://github.com/iwe-org/iwe) (Add; 1,300 stars): Rust LSP + knowledge graph for your markdown notes. Understands document structure (parent-child hierarchy via inclusion links, cross-references). AI agent integration server lets Claude/Codex/Gemini search, read, and update notes with full structural context. Graph traversal: backlinks, parent context, section extract/inline. CLI: squash, export DOT graph, analyse. Works in VS Code, Neovim, Zed, Helix. v0.1.2. 56 releases.
- [graphthulhu](https://github.com/skridlevsky/graphthulhu) (Add; 171 stars): Go MCP server. Full read-write access to Logseq and Obsidian knowledge graphs. 39 tools: navigate (get_page with full block trees, BFS path traversal), search (full-text with parent chain context, DataScript/Datalog for Logseq), analyze (orphan detection, topic clusters, knowledge gaps), write (create/update/delete/move/rename with backlink propagation), decision protocol (check/create/resolve/defer), journal range queries, flashcard SRS (Logseq), whiteboard inspection (Logseq). File watching keeps index in sync. v0.5.0. go install or binary download.
- [mfs](https://github.com/zilliztech/mfs) (Add; 103 stars): Unifies every context source under one URI scheme and one verb set. Two skills: mfs-ingest (mfs add registers/syncs any source), mfs-find (search, grep, ls, cat, tree, head, tail across what is ingested). Connectors: local files, S3/R2/GCS/MinIO, Google Drive, Postgres, MySQL, MongoDB, BigQuery, Snowflake, GitHub (files+issues+PRs), Jira, Linear, HubSpot, Zendesk, Slack, Discord, Gmail, Feishu, Notion, crawled web. --all fans one query across every registered source. Default stack is fully local: ONNX embedding (BGE-M3 int8, ~600MB one-time download, no API key/GPU/cloud), Milvus Lite (local file), SQLite metadata — scales to self-hosted Milvus or Zilliz Cloud + Postgres for production. Credential TOMLs hold only env:/file: references, never raw secrets — real values resolve server-side. 70+ agent integrations via npx skills add. Python/TS/Rust SDKs for building on top. v0.4.3 (installer), 146 commits. 3rd iteration of a Zilliz lineage (claude-context, memsearch).
- [obsidian-second-brain](https://github.com/eugeniughelbur/obsidian-second-brain) (Watch; 3,300 stars): Claude Code skill (installed to ~/.claude/skills/) that turns your Obsidian vault into a persistent second brain. 43 commands. /obsidian-architect (v0.10, May 2026): scans your codebase and writes maintained architecture notes into your vault — refreshable, never overwrites your edits. /obsidian-save, /obsidian-research, /obsidian-recall, /sunday weekly review, /log capture. AI-first vault design: machine-readable frontmatter, mandatory wikilinks, recency markers optimised for Claude retrieval. Google Calendar integration. Anti-hallucination guards. 1,000+ stars in 7 weeks.

### Library documentation retrieval

- [context (neuledge)](https://github.com/neuledge/context) (Add; 333 stars): Community registry of 100+ versioned library doc packages. Local SQLite FTS5, <10ms queries.
- [jdocmunch-mcp](https://github.com/jgravelle/jdocmunch-mcp) (Add; 187 stars): Indexes any documentation set once by heading hierarchy; agents retrieve exact sections via stable byte-offset IDs rather than reading entire files. 10 MCP tools: index_local, index_repo, get_toc, get_toc_tree, search_sections, get_section, get_sections + more. SHA-256 drift detection. Security filtering. _meta envelope reports tokens saved per call. 2.3k LobeHub installs. Python/pip install or uvx. v0.1.1.
- [githits-cli](https://github.com/githits-com/githits-cli) (Add; 71 stars): Version-aware index of the entire public open-source ecosystem, hosted on GitHits infrastructure (Rust indexer, Elixir coordination, Python agentic layer). Local MCP server + CLI let agents search/read/grep the exact source of any npm/PyPI/Crates/NuGet/Maven/Go/RubyGems/Hex/Packagist package at the installed version, without cloning. get_example: cross-repository prior-art search ("HTTP retries with exponential backoff in Python") sourced from repos, issues, discussions, PRs. pkg_vulns, pkg_deps, pkg_changelog, pkg_upgrade_review for dependency evidence. docs_list/docs_read for hosted+repo-backed documentation. License filtering: strict (default, excludes copyleft/undeclared) / custom (account blocklist) / yolo (disabled). 19 supported coding tools including Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI. v0.6.1, 48 releases, 516 commits.

### Codebase understanding & onboarding

- [Understand Anything](https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything) (Add; 74,000 stars): 6-agent LLM pipeline → knowledge graph + interactive dashboard. Guided tours, domain/business logic view, diff impact, committable graph. v2.5.0. Claude Code (native plugin), Cursor, Copilot, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Cline, Hermes, KIMI, and 5 more platforms (14 total). ⚠ Uses Claude Code plugin system as primary install on Claude Code but works on 14 platforms via install.sh.
- [CodeBoarding](https://github.com/CodeBoarding/CodeBoarding) (Add; 2,300 stars): Static analysis + LLM pipeline generating interactive architecture diagrams and component docs committed to .codeboarding/. CLI, VS Code extension, Open VSX extension, GitHub Action. Incremental updates (--incremental, --partial-component-id). Mermaid output for docs and PRs. 800+ public repos visualised (codeboarding.org/diagrams). Supports Python, TS, JS, Java, Go, PHP. All major LLM providers. v0.9.5.

### Code navigation — pick primary; others stackable

- [GitNexus](https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus) (Best in class; 44,000 stars): Zero-server code intelligence engine. Client-side KG in browser. CLI: gitnexus analyze → LadybugDB (not KuzuDB). 16 MCP tools. --skills generates CLAUDE.md skill files per codebase area (Leiden community detection). Hybrid BM25+semantic search. Embeddings (Snowflake arctic-embed-xs). Global registry, multi-repo, no per-project MCP config. Enterprise tier. Cosign SLSA supply-chain attestations. PolyForm NC (free personal/OSS).
- [codebase-memory-mcp](https://github.com/DeusData/codebase-memory-mcp) (Add; 32,000 stars): Structural graph queries: call chains / cross-svc HTTP / dead code / Cypher multi-hop. 155 languages. SLSA Level 3. v0.6.0: now adds semantic search, SIMILAR_TO edges, cross-language intelligence. npm/PyPI/Homebrew/Scoop/Winget install.
- [code-review-graph](https://github.com/tirth8205/code-review-graph) (MIT fallback for GitNexus detect_changes; 20,000 stars): Blast-radius + risk scoring for changed files. v2.3.3. 23 languages, 572 tests, 1400 forks.
- [reposcry](https://github.com/zibouddd/reposcry) (Watch; 13 stars): Rust-native local code graph built around a fast edit loop. 5 binaries: main CLI, incremental updater (reposcry-update), watch mode (reposcry-watch), graph exporter (reposcry-export), expanded MCP server (reposcry-mcp-plus). CRG-compatible commands: get_architecture_overview, query_graph, get_impact_radius, get_affected_flows, semantic_search_nodes, refactor_tool. Install templates for 19 AI platforms. Multiple semantic backends (Ollama, fastembed, Candle/Qwen3). SQLite cache. v0.1.3. Parser-backed: Rust, TS, JS, Python, JSON, TOML, YAML. File-level only: Go, Java, C#, C/C++ (planned).
- [sourcebot](https://github.com/sourcebot-dev/sourcebot) (Add; 3,600 stars): Self-hosted code intelligence platform (Docker Compose + PostgreSQL + Redis). Ask Sourcebot: NL questions answered with inline citations via reasoning model + code search. Blazingly fast code search across all repos/branches (Zoekt trigram engine, regex/boolean/filter support). IDE-level code nav: goto definition, find references. Built-in file explorer with syntax highlighting. Official MCP server for agent integration. Supports GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Gerrit, Bitbucket. 144 releases, v4.17.2. 469K Docker pulls.
- [SocratiCode](https://github.com/giancarloerra/SocratiCode) (Add if you have structured non-code files; 3,100 stars): Hybrid semantic search + structured artifacts (SQL schemas, OpenAPI specs, Terraform); Docker-managed Qdrant
- [graphify](https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify) (Add if you have unstructured docs; 86,000 stars): Multimodal graph: code + PDFs + diagrams + video/audio + SQL schemas. 50+ languages. v8 active branch. Daily commits.
- [codegraph](https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph) (Add; 60,000 stars): Pre-indexed SQLite code knowledge graph. One file per repo, auto-syncs on changes. Benchmarked (2026-06-02 on Opus 4.8, headless, 7 repos, 4 runs/arm, median): 58% fewer tool calls, 22% faster, file reads ≈ zero. 8 agent integrations. npx @colbymchenry/codegraph or codegraph init. MIT.
- [tokensave](https://github.com/aovestdipaperino/tokensave) (Add; 433 stars): Self-disclosed Rust rewrite of codegraph (colbymchenry/codegraph) with substantially expanded scope. 80+ MCP tools (tokensave_search, tokensave_context, tokensave_callers/callees, tokensave_impact, tokensave_dead_code, tokensave_circular, tokensave_type_hierarchy, tokensave_health/gini/dsm code-health analytics, 4 atomic edit primitives with auto re-index, tokensave_diff_context/commit_context/pr_context git integration, tokensave_test_map, multi-branch tools). 50+ languages in 3 tiers (lite/medium/full) via bundled tree-sitter grammars. 16 agent integrations (Claude, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Cline, Roo Code, Zed, Antigravity, Kiro, Kimi, Vibe, Grok, Pi, and more) with per-agent native config. Multi-branch indexing (opt-in per-branch DBs, cross-branch diff/search — unique in this comparison). Subprocess-isolated tree-sitter extraction: a crashing grammar kills only a worker, sync continues. On-demand staleness check (30s cooldown) + catch-up sync on connect, no background daemon or file watcher (removed in 6.1.0 after CPU/memory issues on monorepos). Reproducible benchmark: tokensave bench (93% mean retrieval savings, 10 published queries) + criterion micro-benchmarks against 4 pinned real-world repos (polkadot-sdk, emacs, scipy, node). Per-call token/cost tracking, live TUI monitor, cost parsed from Claude Code session transcripts (13 task categories). libSQL (SQLite fork) storage. v7.0.2, 133+ releases.
- [CodeGraphContext](https://github.com/CodeGraphContext/CodeGraphContext) (Add; 3,900 stars): Python MCP + CLI. 14 languages, KùzuDB/FalkorDB/Neo4j. Pre-indexed bundles. 78 contributors. v0.3.1.
- [contextplus](https://github.com/ForLoopCodes/contextplus) (Watch; 2,000 stars): TypeScript MCP server. Tree-sitter AST (43 extensions) + Spectral Clustering + Ollama/OpenAI embeddings + Obsidian-style wikilink feature hubs. 10 tools: context tree, file skeleton, semantic search, semantic navigation, blast radius, static analysis (TS/Python/Rust/Go), propose_commit (shadow restore points before every AI write), feature hub navigator, memory graph (create_memory_node, create_memory_edge, graph_traverse). bunx contextplus, no install needed.
- [Serena](https://github.com/oraios/serena) (Watch; 26,000 stars): LSP-backed symbol retrieval and symbol-level editing. v1.3.0. Known memory leak (~30GB reported).
- [pharos-mcp](https://github.com/LoganBresnahan/pharos-mcp) (Watch; 4 stars): LSP-MCP bridge. Gives any MCP agent the same language servers your IDE uses (rust-analyzer, gopls, tsserver, pyright, jdtls + 18 more). 53 MCP tools: hover, goto_definition, find_references, call_hierarchy, type_hierarchy, workspace_symbols, get_diagnostics, rename_preview, semantic_tokens, inlay_hints, project memory store + debug/raw escape hatches. Multi-server routing (pyright+ruff for Python). Gleam/OTP BEAM runtime. Self-contained binary via Burrito, npm install. v0.1.0 released 26 May 2026. Benchmark: +7-29pp accuracy, -35-46% wall time vs grep baseline across 5 languages, 1986 cells.
- [jCodemunch](https://github.com/jgravelle/jcodemunch-mcp) (Add if non-commercial; 2,000 stars): tree-sitter AST index + BM25 symbol search + fuzzy matching + optional semantic/hybrid search. find_importers, get_blast_radius, get_class_hierarchy, find_dead_code, get_ranked_context (token-budgeted context assembly). Secret-file detection. 25+ languages. 419K MCP installs globally (#145 on PulseMCP). v1.108.25, hyper-active (108+ major revisions). Telemetry: anonymous token savings counter, disable with JCODEMUNCH_SHARE_SAVINGS=0.
- [n2-arachne](https://github.com/choihyunsus/n2-arachne) (Watch; 55 stars): Push model: pre-assembles 4-layer token-budgeted context before agent acts
- [depmesh](https://github.com/Tiendil/depmesh) (Watch; 4 stars): CLI tool making file-to-file relationships an explicit, declared part of project configuration rather than agent-inferred. depmesh.toml defines relations (tested_by, governed_by, imports, imported_by, or any custom relation) via glob pattern rules, fixed lists, CLI commands, or scripts. depmesh dependencies --relation tested_by ./src/app.py returns exact related artifacts deterministically — no AST parsing, no embeddings, no LLM inference. depmesh skill usage/configuration/initialization give agent-oriented docs. Self-dogfoods its own depmesh.toml. BSD-3-Clause, Python 97.1%.
- [codemark](https://github.com/DanielCardonaRojas/codemark) (Watch; 14 stars): Structural code bookmarking system using tree-sitter to capture semantic structure rather than fragile file:line coordinates. Bookmarks self-heal across renames, refactors, and formatting changes via tiered matching (Exact → Relaxed → Hash Fallback). codemark add --file src/auth.rs --range 42 --tag auth --note "JWT entry point" bookmarks the AST node, not the line number. codemark resolve <id> finds it even after the code moved. Semantic search via local vector embeddings (sqlite-vec, no API key): codemark reindex then codemark search --semantic "how are tokens validated?". Collections group bookmarks into task-specific sets. Git-integrated: tracks bookmarks across commits/branches. Integrations: Television (TUI fuzzy-finder), Neovim (gutter signs, visual-selection bookmarking, Telescope), Claude Code (dedicated plugin, badged "agent-ready"). 8 languages via tree-sitter: Rust, Swift, TypeScript/TSX, Python, Go, Java, Dart, C#. SQLite bundled. 112 commits, 10 releases, v0.5.2 (Apr 20 2026), published on crates.io.
- [sdl-mcp](https://github.com/GlitterKill/sdl-mcp) (Add; 440 stars): Symbol Card graph (LadybugDB) with 4-rung Iris Gate Ladder: escalate from 100-token Symbol Card → Skeleton IR → Hot-Path Excerpt → policy-gated raw source. Live indexing (unsaved edits). Graph slicing with weighted BFS. SCIP compiler-grade cross-refs. Cross-session development memories. Tool gateway (38→6 tools). Sandboxed runtime execution (outputMode: minimal). Observability dashboard. v0.11.3.
- [GrapeRoot (Codex-CLI-Compact)](https://github.com/kunal12203/codex-cli-compact) (Add; 930 stars): Dual-graph context engine wrapping AI agent invocations. Run dgc instead of claude. Builds info_graph.json (semantic graph: files, symbols, imports) + chat_action_graph.json (session memory: reads, edits, queries). Pre-loads relevant files per prompt — agent reasons, not explores. Compounding context: each turn cheaper as graph fills in. MCP tools: graph_read, graph_retrieve, graph_neighbors, count_tokens, get_session_stats. Token dashboard at localhost:8899. Cross-session context-store.json. Supports Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Copilot. v3.9.90 (rapid PyPI versioning). Benchmarks: $0.46→$0.27 (41% cheaper), 16.8→10.3 turns, 82.7→87.1 quality across 80+ prompts.

### Architecture violation detection

- [repo-graph](https://github.com/andreirx/repo-graph) (Watch; 11 stars): Deterministic graph with trust model: classifies unresolved edges, reliability surface, obligation/CI gate, trend analysis. CLI only. No releases.
- [TrueCourse](https://github.com/truecourse-ai/truecourse) (Watch; 496 stars): Circular deps, layer violations, race conditions, Prisma/Drizzle ER diagrams, LLM semantic code review, git diff mode. v0.2.4.

### MCP definition tokens

- [Tool Search](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) (Add; — stars): Defers tool schema loading until needed; auto-triggers above 10% context threshold
- [mcpick](https://github.com/spences10/mcpick) (Watch; 91 stars): Manual CLI toggle of MCP servers per session / profile

### Agent memory persistence

- [mem0](https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0) (Add; 61,000 stars): Universal memory layer for AI agents. Extracts atomic facts from conversations and persists them scoped to user, session, or agent. On next session injects relevant memories — agent knows your preferences and history without re-reading full conversation history. Native .claude-plugin and .cursor-plugin integrations. OpenMemory: free self-hosted Docker path. MCP server. Python + TypeScript SDKs. 277 releases, v1.0.9. arXiv:2504.19413.
- [cognee](https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee) (Add; 28,000 stars): Knowledge graph memory engine. Four operations: remember, recall, forget, improve. Dual layer: fast session cache + persistent graph with background sync. Claude Code plugin: captures tool calls into session memory via hooks, syncs to graph at session end. cognee-mcp developer rules bootstrap: indexes .cursorrules, .cursor/rules, AGENT.md into developer_rules nodeset — queryable rather than loaded wholesale. MCP server with HTTP/SSE/stdio transports. ~12k stars, v1.1.0-dev.
- [supermemory](https://github.com/supermemoryai/supermemory) (Add; 28,000 stars): SaaS-hosted memory API for AI agents. Dedicated Claude Code plugin: injects user profile (preferences, recent activity) at session start; auto-captures conversation turns for future recall. MCP server at mcp.supermemory.ai. Scoped by project container tags. Team Memory across repos. Browser extension. Also plugins for OpenCode, OpenClaw, Hermes. npm install supermemory or pip install supermemory. Personal use free.
- [gitmem](https://github.com/dev-boz/gitmem) (Watch; 33 stars): Git-native shared memory for AI coding agents. Memory = markdown files in git repos, synced through GitHub, governed via PRs. Fixes the multi-tool problem: Claude Code + Codex + Copilot + Gemini + OpenCode share the same memory. Encoding strength 1-5 (AST-parsed fact S:4 outranks LLM inference S:2 — hard rule not scoring). Dream pipeline: orient, gather, consolidate, lint, prune on free LLM quota. PR governance: cheap models propose, SotA models review, humans confirm. Provenance tracking. Cognitive science taxonomy (19 references in spec). Zero infrastructure — pure git + markdown.
- [memoir](https://github.com/zhangfengcdt/memoir) (Watch; 593 stars): Git-like version control for AI agent memory. Semantic hierarchical paths (profile.professional.skills.python) instead of UUID keys. O(log n) lookups. Branch-aware memory: git checkout to feature branch → agent uses branch-appropriate memories, no experimental patterns bleeding into production. Solves token rent: targeted injection preserves prefix cache vs CLAUDE.md global invalidation. memoir blame/checkout for auditing. Cryptographic integrity. Visual explorer (Tree/Graph/Timeline/Places). Native Claude Code plugin via .claude-plugin/. Hooks for session-start, user-prompt-submit, stop. v0.1.9. 145 commits.
- [claude-mem](https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem) (Add; 87,000 stars): Claude Code-native persistent memory. 5 lifecycle hooks (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, Stop, SessionEnd) capture tool-use observations during sessions, compress via Claude agent SDK, store in SQLite + Chroma vector DB. Worker service: HTTP API port 37777, web viewer UI, 10 search endpoints, managed by Bun. mem-search skill for natural language queries with progressive disclosure. 4 MCP tools in 3-layer workflow: search (compact index, ~50-100 tokens/result) → timeline (chronological context) → get_observations (full details, ~500-1000 tokens/result, only for filtered IDs) — ~10x token savings vs fetching everything upfront. Privacy: <private> tags exclude content from storage. Beta channel: Endless Mode (biomimetic memory for extended sessions). Multi-language modes (zh, ja). Works with Claude Code, OpenClaw, Codex, Gemini, Hermes, Copilot, OpenCode. v13.2.0, 270 releases.
- [claude-smart](https://github.com/ReflexioAI/claude-smart) (Add; 745 stars): Self-improvement plugin for Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode that turns interactions into durable skills. Distinction from memory tools: LEARNING (actionable rules Claude follows) not MEMORY (transcript of what happened). Three artifacts auto-built: Preferences (project-scoped, how you work here), Project-specific skills (triggered rules with rationales), Shared skills (cross-project rollups). Self-tuning library: correct same thing twice → rules merge; change mind → old archived, new supersedes; near-duplicates consolidate. Hybrid vector + BM25 search via in-process ONNX embedder (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, ~86MB, no external API). Dashboard at localhost:3001. Citation markers in replies. /claude-smart:learn, /show, /dashboard commands. Powered by Reflexio engine (separate Apache-2.0 project). 45 tags, v0.2.47. Benchmarked vs claude-mem: ~2.7x higher overall quality, ~3x better correction retention (EXPERIMENT.md). GDPVal benchmark on Reflexio engine: -81% planning steps, -72% tokens.
- [TrueMemory](https://github.com/buildingjoshbetter/TrueMemory) (Add; 366 stars): Local-first agent memory in one SQLite file. Three tiers: Edge (fastest, runs anywhere), Base (strongest fully-offline, 88.2% LoCoMo), Pro (adds HyDE query expansion via LLM API, 93.0% LoCoMo). Lifecycle hooks auto-capture conversations; memories processed/consolidated on session end ("dreaming"). Directives: m.add(content, directive=True) for standing instructions auto-loaded every session — a per-memory CLAUDE.md alternative. search_deep: agentic multi-round search for ambiguous queries. Benchmarked on LoCoMo (1540 q), LongMemEval (500 q), and BEAM-1M/BEAM-10M (novel: tests memory recall across conversations exceeding any model context window, up to 10M tokens / ~20,000 messages). Works with Claude Code, Claude CLI, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Claude Desktop. Rebranded from "Neuromem". v0.7.1.2+, hyperactive releases.
- [agentic-context-engine (ACE)](https://github.com/kayba-ai/agentic-context-engine) (Add; 2,500 stars): Continual learning framework based on Stanford/SambaNova ACE paper and Dynamic Cheatsheet. Maintains a persistent "Skillbook" of learned strategies (not declarative facts) that functions as an evolving system prompt. Three roles: Agent (executes, enhanced by Skillbook), Reflector (analyzes traces — writes/executes Python in sandboxed env to programmatically find patterns, the "Recursive Reflector"), SkillManager (curates Skillbook). No fine-tuning, no training data, no vector database. Runners: ACELiteLLM (any of 100+ LiteLLM providers), Core (batch epochs), TraceAnalyser (learn from existing logs), browser-use, LangChain, ClaudeCode (Claude Code CLI tasks with learning). Optional MCP server (ace-framework[mcp]) and embedding-based deduplication. Composable pipeline: AgentStep→EvaluateStep→ReflectStep→UpdateStep→DeduplicateStep. Optional hosted SaaS at kayba.ai (free trial). v0.12.0, 32 releases.
- [mem](https://github.com/karmugilen/mem) (Watch; 1 stars): Daily journal built on the "latent memory" thesis: LLM weights already encode general world knowledge, so the tool only needs to store the tiny cues the model cannot invent — your specific decisions and why. One plain-markdown file per day (~/mem/journal/YYYY-MM-DD.md), no database, no cloud, no vector embeddings, no LLM extraction step. Journal is shared across ALL projects by default (project: prefix per line), not siloed per-repo. Commands: mem log, mem task/tasks/done, mem last 7 (restore a week + open tasks), mem search, mem edit. Auto-commits every write to git as a lightweight backup. Piped command output auto-trimmed to cue + evidence (e.g. pytest 2>&1 | mem log "myapp: tests: 3 fail, all JWT expiry"). Installs Claude/Grok/AGENTS.md instruction snippets + a SKILL.md (also mirrored at skills/mem/ for skill-marketplace discovery) during setup. Tri-platform from day one: PowerShell 31.5% / Python 31.1% / Shell 31.0%.
- [curion](https://github.com/geanatz/curion) (Watch; 31 stars): Deliberately minimal project-local memory MCP server. Entire public API is two frozen tools: remember(text) stores a piece of project memory, recall(text) retrieves relevant pieces — no kinds, states, filters, or knobs. README states explicitly: "the two tools, their strict input schemas, and the public text/structuredContent surfaces are stable and frozen." Raw input is never persisted — a controller-normalization + safety-filter layer runs before write; only normalized summaries and metadata (kind, confidence, safety flags, timestamps) land in local SQLite (.curion/curion.sqlite, gitignored). stdio transport only — curion opens no network sockets itself; the MCP host owns the network boundary as subprocess spawn. Opt-in semantic retrieval can search across other non-private projects on the same machine; a project marked isPrivate:true in .curion/config.json is never surfaced to cross-project search. Pluggable provider: OpenAI-compatible (default) or Anthropic. Does not load .env files — config passed via parent process env only. Full docs tree (mcp-clients, configuration, reference, privacy-storage), SECURITY.md, CHANGELOG (Keep a Changelog format). 4 releases, v0.3.6 (Jun 30 2026), 71 commits.

### Cross-session governance & reasoning capture

- [Bitloops](https://github.com/bitloops/bitloops) (Watch; 230 stars): Rust daemon: captures developer-agent reasoning linked to git commits. Shared repo-scoped memory across agents. DevQL typed GraphQL interface. External knowledge (GitHub issues/PRs, Jira, Confluence). Local dashboard. SQLite+DuckDB default backends. Semantic embeddings (local or OpenAI). Supports Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Copilot, OpenCode. brew install available. v0.0.17. Telemetry opt-in at first init.
- [Aura](https://github.com/Naridon-Inc/aura) (Watch; 36 stars): AST Merkle-Graph on git; intent gating, fn-level rewind, compressed handover
- [aictx](https://github.com/oldskultxo/aictx) (Watch; 54 stars): Repo-local continuity runtime. Solves agent cold-start: each session starts from the operational state left by prior work via two CLI calls — aictx resume (produces resume capsule with active task, next action, known failures, verified strategies, branch-safe Work State) and aictx finalize (captures execution evidence). Core capabilities: Work State (task, hypothesis, files, next action, risks), Failure Memory (structured patterns of observed command/test/build/lint failures — agents avoid repeating them), Strategy Memory (reusable successful execution patterns), Handoffs/Decisions (survive session boundaries), RepoMap (optional Tree-sitter structural map). Claude-aware (.claude/hooks/aictx_*.py), Codex-first, generic-agent compatible. All artifacts local JSON/Markdown in .aictx/ directory. No model calls. 28 releases, v5.3.0. Demo: 5 vs 10 files explored, 8 vs 15 commands, 1m12s vs 1m59s (honest framing: not a universal benchmark).
- [greplica](https://github.com/Autoloops/greplica) (Add; 313 stars): Graph-structured memory of engineering knowledge (components, flows, decisions, constraints) for coding agents. Two skills: greplica-bootstrap (once, repo start) and greplica-update-working-memory (end of sessions with durable decisions). greplica graph context "<question>" retrieves relevant prior context before broad manual exploration — reduces repeated rg searches and rediscovery of past decisions. Retrieval combines keyword search, semantic embeddings, and graph traversal. Local embedding mode available (no API key) or OpenAI embeddings. Supports Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot CLI (Cline in progress). Stored at ~/.greplica/graph.db, no cloud sync, no telemetry. Benchmarked on SWE-chat (public dataset + paper): held-out planning tasks, clean base snapshot + 2-4 prior sessions build context, baseline vs Greplica-context run compared. Best case 75% fewer tokens + 38% faster; typical 40-50% token reduction. Company-backed (Autoloops).
- [graphiti](https://github.com/getzep/graphiti) (Watch; 29,000 stars): Temporal context graph engine. Every fact has a validity window — when it became true and when superseded. Agents can query "what was true on Tuesday" not just "what is true now". Incremental graph construction (no batch recomputation). Hybrid retrieval: semantic + BM25 + graph traversal. mcp_server/ directory for Claude Code and Cursor integration. 194 releases, v0.29.0 Apr 2026. arXiv:2501.13956. Backends: Neo4j, FalkorDB (Docker), KùzuDB, Amazon Neptune.

### Response verbosity & memory compression

- [caveman](https://github.com/JuliusBrussee/caveman) (Add; 89,000 stars): Skill strips output filler; companion compresses CLAUDE.md (~45% savings). 30+ agents. QLoRA fine-tune. Run after sigmap.

### Config stack audit & optimisation

- [token-optimizer](https://github.com/alexgreensh/token-optimizer) (Add; 1,600 stars): Claude Code skill that dispatches 6 parallel audit agents across your full config stack: CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, skills, MCP servers, commands, rules, .claudeignore. Measures per-message overhead, identifies what is eating context, implements fixes with backup + diff approval. Skill usage analytics (tracks which of your 59 installed skills you actually use). Session health monitoring. Interactive HTML dashboard. Python measurement script with before/after comparison. Local SQLite trend tracking. No releases yet.
- [ctxharness](https://github.com/FlorianBruniaux/ctxharness) (Add; 18 stars): CLI/CI tool that validates AI instruction files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules) for drift and quality. L1 fact drift: version numbers in docs vs actual package.json/Cargo.toml/go.mod/Prisma schema (20 extractors). L2 instruction quality: vague language detection, positive/negative ratio, token budget with @file.md chain resolution. L3 context assembly: hook validation, skill frontmatter checks, rule glob validity, coverage ratio. ctxharness fix --apply auto-patches stale versions. ctxharness score gives 0-100 health score. ctxharness diff blocks CI on regression. GitHub Action available. Stack presets for T3, Next.js, Python, Go, Rust. v0.4.1. MIT. Made by RTK author.
- [tokentelemetry](https://github.com/VasiHemanth/tokentelemetry) (Add; 232 stars): Local, zero-config observability dashboard (Next.js frontend + FastAPI backend) reading log files that AI coding agents already write to disk — no SDK, no instrumentation, no cloud account. Unified dashboard: token usage/cost per session-model-project, tool-call waterfall traces, reasoning chain capture, plan-mode capture, per-model efficiency comparison, per-project budget limits (observational, never blocks agents), cost anomaly detection (flags silent reasoning-token waste). Dedicated Hermes Agent dashboard (Nous Research autonomous-agent framework): gateway health, cron jobs, skills, memory, subagent delegation traces across 38 source platforms — the only tool in this comparison covering a non-coding autonomous agent. Reads logs from 10 coding agents: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Qwen CLI, OpenCode, Vibe, Antigravity, Grok Build. Separate Hermes Dashboard plugin for embedding as a tab. 67 commits, no formal releases yet.

### Agent safety enforcement

- [Sponsio](https://github.com/SponsioLabs/Sponsio) (Watch; 465 stars): Runtime contract enforcement for AI agents. Write rules in plain English; Sponsio compiles to LTL then DFA and checks every tool call in <0.01ms with zero LLM cost. Pre-built bundles covering all 10 OWASP Agentic Top 10 risks. Blocks credential-scope abuse, destructive API calls, runaway loops. Replays real incidents (PocketOS prod-DB wipe, CVE-2025-55284). Framework-agnostic: LangChain, Claude Agent, OpenAI Agents, MCP, CrewAI, Vercel AI. Python + TypeScript SDK. v0.1.0 alpha.
- [faramesh-core](https://github.com/faramesh/faramesh-core) (Add; 95 stars): Pre-execution governance engine. Sits between agent and tools; evaluates every tool call against FPL policy (PERMIT/DEFER/DENY) via 11-step deterministic pipeline with no LLM in the decision path. faramesh mcp wrap intercepts every MCP tools/call before it reaches the real server. Credential broker strips API keys and mints short-lived scoped credentials per permitted call. Kernel enforcement on Linux (seccomp-BPF, Landlock, netns). SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity. Tamper-evident WAL with Ed25519 signing. 13 framework auto-patches (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Claude Code, MCP, OpenAI Agents, etc). Policy packs. v1.2.6. arXiv 2601.17744.

### Agent runtime & context orchestration

- [ark](https://github.com/atripati/ark) (Watch; 39 stars): Go agent runtime kernel. Dynamically loads only 3-5 tools per task (99.9% reduction: 60,468 tokens raw → ~80 tokens). 6-signal weighted scoring: relevance (40%), success rate (30%), cost (-15%), latency (-5%), confidence (10%), memory bonus. Online learning persists across restarts (ark-memory.json). Adaptive execution: tool-not-found → load more; tool-misunderstood → upgrade schema; tool-crashed → swap. Decision-level cost attribution per step/tool/action. Grounding gate (zero hallucination across 30-run stress test). Safety: domain allowlist, write protection, dry-run. 67 tests, race detector clean. v0.7 current. Go 1.22+, Apache-2.0.

### Universal context compression middleware

- [headroom](https://github.com/headroomlabs-ai/headroom) (Add; 59,000 stars): Compresses everything before it reaches the LLM: tool outputs, logs, RAG chunks, files, conversation history. 7 algorithms: SmartCrusher (JSON), CodeCompressor (AST), Kompress-base (custom HuggingFace model), image compression, CacheAligner (stabilises KV cache prefixes), IntelligentContext, CCR (reversible — originals stored locally, LLM retrieves on demand). 4 deployment modes: headroom wrap claude, proxy (port 8787, zero code changes), library, MCP server (headroom_compress, headroom_retrieve, headroom_stats). Cross-agent memory. headroom learn: mines failed sessions, writes to CLAUDE.md. 60B+ community tokens saved. v0.22.4. 152 releases. Ships RTK internally.
- [ContextForge](https://github.com/anujkushwaha612/ContextForge) (Watch; 7 stars): Local proxy + CLI sitting between Claude Code and any LLM provider. cf wrap claude starts proxy, indexes repo (Tree-sitter AST: JS/TS/TSX/Python/Go/Rust/Java), launches Claude Code through it. Protocol translation lets Claude Code drive Ollama, OpenAI, Groq, Gemini — not just Anthropic. Repository Graph: find_symbol, analyze_impact, find_route (SQLite + HNSW vector index). Context Optimizer: tool-schema minimization, keep-newest dedup (SimHash near-dup detection), age-gated AST skeletonization, junk vaulting (lockfiles, minified bundles, base64 blobs). Surgical AST-aware patching returns unified diff (no re-read to verify). Per-workspace persistent session memory (HNSW + SQLite). Live dashboard at localhost:3000. cf doctor: 8-point install diagnosis with --fix. Prebuilt native binaries for 5 platforms. v1.0.0 released 4 Jul 2026, 23 commits.

### Tabular data retrieval

- [jdatamunch-mcp](https://github.com/jgravelle/jdatamunch-mcp) (Watch; 70 stars): Indexes CSV/Excel files once into SQLite; agents query column profiles, filtered rows, and server-side aggregations instead of loading entire files. 9 MCP tools: index_local, describe_dataset, describe_column, search_data, get_rows (10 filter operators), aggregate (GROUP BY in SQLite), sample_rows, list_datasets, get_session_stats. Token savings telemetry per call. 25,333x reduction benchmark on 1M-row CSV (111M→3,849 tokens, measured). Incremental re-index on unchanged files (hash check). v0.1.1.

### Reference resource (curated list)

- [awesome-second-brain](https://github.com/aristoapp/awesome-second-brain) (Reference only; 468 stars): Curated comparison of second brain, AI memory, and knowledge systems. Covers Collect/Organize/Evolve/Use/Govern lifecycle. Compares Membase, OpenHuman, GBrain, Supermemory, Mem0/OpenMemory, Zep/Graphiti, Cognee, Khoj, Obsidian+AI bridge, ChatGPT Memory, Claude Projects, NotebookLM. Deep-dives: capability matrix, setup burden, agent activation channels, local vs cloud, personal vs team. 0 releases, 21 commits.
- [Awesome-AI-Memory](https://github.com/IAAR-Shanghai/Awesome-AI-Memory) (Reference only; 1,100 stars): Bilingual (Chinese primary, English via README_en.md) continuously-updated curated knowledge base on AI/LLM/agent memory: long-term memory, reasoning, retrieval, memory-native system design. Covers research papers, engineering frameworks, system designs, evaluation benchmarks, real-world application practices. Maintained by IAAR-Shanghai (Institute for Advanced Algorithms Research, Shanghai) — an institutional AI research organization, not an anonymous account. 312 commits, no releases (rolling updates). Referenced by at least one other awesome-list as a primary source.
- [awesome-agent-vault](https://github.com/zriyansh/awesome-agent-vault) (Reference only; 13 stars): Category map for agent credential management: vaults, proxies, identity layers, credential gateways. Five reader-intent buckets: Products (vaults/proxies you install), Integrations (how Claude Code/Codex/Cursor/LangChain etc. consume credentials today), Services (per-API recipes: Stripe, GitHub, Slack scoping guidance), Patterns (named recipes: lethal trifecta, just-in-time injection, subagent non-inheritance), Threat models (prompt-injection credential disclosure, shell history exposure, offline disk-image theft). Compatibility matrix across 10 agent platforms x 15 vault products. Curated by Authsome (agentrhq) — a credential-vault vendor whose own product is listed among direct competitors. CC0 1.0 (public domain). "Last verified: 2026-05-27." 5 commits.
- [awesome-harness-engineering](https://github.com/ai-boost/awesome-harness-engineering) (Reference only; 3,100 stars): Comprehensive curated list for the entire discipline of AI agent harness engineering: the scaffolding (context delivery, tool interfaces, planning artifacts, verification loops, memory systems, sandboxes) surrounding an agent. Sections: Foundations (canonical essays from Anthropic/OpenAI/Google/Martin Fowler on what harness engineering is), Agent Loop, Planning & Task Decomposition, Context Delivery & Compaction (directly cites context-mode, headroom, codebase-memory-mcp — tools already in this comparison — plus many not yet assessed here: LLMLingua, Token Savior, Trellis, OpenViking, Mirage, dirac, MinishLab/semble), Tool Design, Skills & MCP, Permissions & Authorization, Memory & State (Letta/MemGPT, mem0, Zep, engram, Stash, TencentDB-Agent-Memory), Task Runners & Orchestration, Verification & CI, Observability, Debugging, Human-in-the-Loop, Reference Implementations, Security/Sandbox, Evals, Templates. Substantive one-paragraph annotation per entry, not bare links. 166 commits, 81 open PRs — very active community contribution.

### Code generation minimalism (YAGNI enforcement)

- [ponytail](https://github.com/DietrichGebert/ponytail) (Add; 83,000 stars): Skill/plugin enforcing a YAGNI decision ladder before the agent writes any code: (1) does this need to exist — skip if not, (2) does stdlib do it, (3) native platform feature, (4) installed dependency, (5) one line, (6) only then the minimum that works. Never cuts validation, error handling, security, or accessibility. Commands: /ponytail [lite|full|ultra|off], /ponytail-review (diff over-engineering check), /ponytail-audit (whole-repo audit), /ponytail-debt (harvest deferred shortcuts into a ledger), /ponytail-gain (benchmark scoreboard). 14 platforms: Claude Code, Codex, Copilot CLI, Pi, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, CodeWhale, OpenClaw, plus instruction-only Cursor/Windsurf/Cline/Kiro. Agentic benchmark (real Claude Code session, real FastAPI+React repo, 12 tasks, n=4): -54% LOC, -22% tokens, -20% cost, -27% time vs no-skill baseline, 100% safe. v4.7.0, 9 releases, created 12 Jun 2026.

---

These ratings are decision support. A human should make and document any
adoption decision that materially affects a team or product.
